args.content: Recovered brownfield baseline.

documentation.value: Describe the element as a goal: a high-level end state or outcome that stakeholders intend to achieve. In this architecture, Product Delivery Goal defines the intended strategic outcome for Current Product Goal and anchors the rest of the baseline intention model. Recovered brownfield baseline.

Describe the element as a capability: an ability that an organization, person, or system possesses to achieve an outcome. In this architecture, Core Product Capability captures the organization or product's capacity to achieve Current Product Goal through coordinated business and application behavior.

Describe the element as a course of action: an approach, directive, or plan for configuring capabilities and resources. In this architecture, MVP Delivery Course Of Action prescribes a runnable local MVP with explicit architecture-to-implementation traceability as the preferred means of delivery.

Describe the element as a business actor: an organizational entity that is capable of performing behavior and owning or using business roles, processes, or services. In this architecture, Primary User identifies the main human or organizational party that consumes the delivered business service and interacts with the core business flow.

Describe the element as a business process: a sequence of business behavior that produces a defined set of business outcomes. In this architecture, Core Business Flow sequences the core activities needed to operationalize Current Product Goal and deliver the intended value to the primary user.

Describe the element as a business service: externally visible business behavior delivered to customers or other business roles. In this architecture, Core User Service is the user-facing service delivered through the core business process to the primary actor.

Describe the element as an application component: a modular application building block that encapsulates automated behavior and data. In this architecture, Core Application System is the modular software building block that automates and supports the business process behind Current Product Goal.

Describe the element as an application service: externally visible automated behavior provided by an application component. In this architecture, Core Application Service provides the externally visible automation boundary that supports and serves the core business service.

Describe the element as a data object: passive data suitable for automated processing by application behavior. In this architecture, Core Domain Data captures the domain and state information created, read, and maintained by the core application system.

Describe the element as a node: a computational or physical resource that hosts, executes, or supports technology and application behavior. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime is the runtime environment that hosts and executes the technology and application behavior required for Current Product Goal.

Describe the element as an artifact: a physical or deployable piece of information used or produced in technology or application realization. In this architecture, Deployable Solution Artifact is the deployable solution package generated from the core application system for operation on the target runtime node.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Product Capability makes Product Delivery Goal concrete by providing the ability needed to achieve that strategic outcome.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, MVP Delivery Course Of Action gives concrete form to Core Product Capability by prescribing how that capability will be established.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Business Flow operationalizes Core Product Capability through executable business behavior.

Describe the relationship as an assignment: the allocation of responsibility, behavior, or execution from an active structure element to a behavior element, or vice versa where appropriate in ArchiMate. In this architecture, Primary User is allocated participation responsibility in Core Business Flow from a business perspective.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Business Flow implements and makes Core User Service concrete as externally visible behavior.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core User Service provides business value and externally visible behavior to Primary User.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core Application Service provides the automated support needed by the business-facing Core User Service.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Core Application System implements and makes Core Application Service concrete as the exposed application service boundary.

Describe the relationship as an access relationship: the source behavior or active structure concept reads, writes, or otherwise uses the target passive structure concept. In this architecture, Core Application System reads and writes Core Domain Data in order to execute application behavior.

Describe the relationship as an association: a generic structural or semantic link used when no more specific ArchiMate relationship type applies. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime has a structural support link with Core Application System where no more specific baseline relationship is modeled.

Describe the relationship as a serving relationship: the source concept provides functionality or value that is used by the target concept. In this architecture, Core Technology Runtime provides the infrastructure behavior required to host and expose Core Application Service.

Describe the relationship as a realization: the source concept implements, fulfills, or makes the target concept concrete. In this architecture, Deployable Solution Artifact is the concrete deployable manifestation of Core Application System.
Core layered intention model maintained by SystemArchitect.

args.title: Validate Page Access
args.content:
[Input]: Active browser tab URL, optional host permission patterns, stored page rules, and Chrome runtime permission state.
[Processing]: Check whether the page is restricted, whether the domain is within the controllable authorization list, whether an enabled rule matches the page, and whether runtime host permission has been granted before capture or run startup.
[Output]: A page-access decision containing allowed or blocked state, matched-rule context, permission guidance, or a domain error.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Restricted pages are rejected; unmatched rules block capture; unauthorized domains surface an explicit authorization path; authorized matched pages proceed consistently.

args.title: Start Assisted Run From Active Tab
args.content:
[Input]: User prompt, selected main agent, optional session id, capture flag, active-tab metadata, captured page fields, and username context.
[Processing]: Optionally capture page data, derive username context, normalize captured fields, reuse or create the session context, call the adapter start-run API, and initialize run state for downstream SSE streaming.
[Output]: A started run with run id, selected agent, optional session id, and initialized extension run record.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Capture-plus-run and follow-up flows both work; startup failures surface normalized errors; successful startup persists active run and session state for the side panel.

args.title: Synchronize Assistant State
args.content:
[Input]: Partial or full assistant state updates from background actions, run lifecycle changes, and side-panel sync requests.
[Processing]: Merge state with defaults, persist it to chrome.storage.local, timestamp the update, and broadcast STATE_UPDATED so background and side-panel consumers stay aligned.
[Output]: Durable shared extension state for capture, run, error, and session context.
[Acceptance Criteria]: State writes preserve main-agent and run context; broadcasts follow persistence; refreshed UI views observe coherent status and error data.

args.title: Collect Page Fields
args.content:
[Input]: Requested field definitions, authorized document context, window context, DOM selectors, and optional fallback values.
[Processing]: For each enabled field rule, read the configured source such as document title, URL, selected text, meta tag, selector text, or selector attribute, normalize whitespace, and apply fallback values when extraction is empty.
[Output]: A captured field map keyed by configured field identifiers such as software_version, selected_sr, pageTitle, pageUrl, or selectedText.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Enabled configured fields are extracted deterministically; missing values fall back as configured; unauthorized or unavailable pages do not silently fabricate capture results.

args.title: Extract Username Context
args.content:
[Input]: Authorized document and window objects containing DOM attributes, text nodes, meta tags, and optional global user variables.
[Processing]: Search supported DOM selectors first, then configured user-related meta tags, then page-global variables, and normalize the first reliable username evidence with its source classification.
[Output]: A username context containing username text and source such as dom_data_attribute, dom_text, meta_tag, page_global, unknown_fallback, or unresolved_login_state.
[Acceptance Criteria]: DOM-based identity evidence takes precedence over meta and globals; the result includes a provenance label; pages lacking evidence fall back to unknown rather than incorrect identities.

args.title: Inject In-Page Assistant Entrypoint
args.content:
[Input]: Authorized page DOM readiness state and extension runtime messaging capabilities.
[Processing]: Create a floating AI button once per page, wire its click action to request panel opening, and toggle an embedded iframe panel only when side-panel APIs are unavailable and fallback is needed.
[Output]: An in-page assistant entrypoint that opens the side panel or embedded fallback UI for the current business page.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The floating button appears at most once per page; clicking it triggers extension messaging; embedded fallback panels can be opened and closed without duplicating UI containers.

args.title: Consume Normalized Run Stream
args.content:
[Input]: Run id, adapter SSE endpoint, normalized run events, and current side-panel state.
[Processing]: Open the EventSource stream, validate incoming event payloads, merge events into local run history and live state, derive lifecycle status, refresh persisted run summaries, and keep the UI stream status synchronized.
[Output]: An updated live conversation state containing ordered run events, derived run status, final output snapshots, and stream connection state.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Valid events are appended without duplication; terminal evidence updates run status correctly; reconnect states are surfaced; event history persists for later replay.

args.title: Project Transcript Read Model
args.content:
[Input]: Historical run details, live normalized events, answer records, final output snapshots, and prior transcript projection state.
[Processing]: Convert raw events into fragments, classify assistant text versus reasoning and questions, group them into transcript messages, and merge historical plus live segments into a stable conversation read model.
[Output]: A transcript projection suitable for side-panel chat rendering, summaries, and cockpit status displays.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Assistant streamed text remains distinguishable from reasoning; history and live segments merge stably; question and answer turns render in the correct conversational order.

args.title: Submit Question Answer
args.content:
[Input]: Current run id, active normalized question payload, chosen option id and/or free-text answer.
[Processing]: Post the answer to the adapter, persist an AnswerRecord locally, clear pending question state, restore streaming status, and synchronize the updated answer set back to background storage.
[Output]: Accepted question-answer state with persisted answer history and resumed conversation streaming.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Answers are rejected only when the adapter reports failure; accepted answers are stored once; pending-question UI clears and the run returns to streaming state.

args.title: Call Adapter API
args.content:
[Input]: Prompt payloads, capture payloads, username context, selected agent, optional session id, feedback requests, and optional API key configuration.
[Processing]: Build validated HTTP request bodies and headers for run start, answer submission, feedback submission, and SSE stream setup, then parse normalized success or failure responses with schema validation.
[Output]: Typed extension-side API results and stream connections aligned to the adapter contract.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Requests include authentication when configured; invalid or failed responses are normalized; run, answer, and feedback endpoints remain contract-compatible with the adapter.

args.title: Evaluate Rules And Canonical Capture
args.content:
[Input]: Current page URL, stored page rules, field definitions, and raw captured field values.
[Processing]: Match enabled rules using hostname and path wildcards, provide default rule templates, and transform raw capture output into canonical fields such as pageTitle, pageUrl, software_version, selected_sr, and selectedText.
[Output]: Matching-rule decisions plus canonicalized capture payloads used by background and UI workflows.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Rule matching respects hostname-only and pathname-plus-search semantics; default templates remain available; canonical capture output consistently exposes software_version and selected_sr when collected.

args.title: Persist Run History
args.content:
[Input]: Run records, normalized run events, answer records, and session identifiers from active or completed conversations.
[Processing]: Persist run artifacts in browser storage, reload run detail views on demand, group runs into sessions, and keep historical details available after UI refresh or reopen.
[Output]: A durable browser-side run history with per-run detail, answer history, and session navigation views.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Multiple runs remain queryable after side-panel reloads; events and answers stay associated with the correct run; session grouping is stable when session ids are present.

args.title: Broker Run Startup
args.content:
[Input]: RunStartRequest containing prompt, selected agent, optional capture payload, optional session id, and extension context metadata.
[Processing]: Validate the selected main agent, discover the canonical remote agent from /agent, reuse or create an OpenCode session, dispatch prompt_async to the selected session, initialize run state, and record startup diagnostics.
[Output]: A registered adapter run id with selected agent, optional session id, and initial tool-call events that describe startup progress.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Only allowed main agents are accepted; remote /agent discovery must precede session bootstrap; reused sessions skip session creation; startup failures are surfaced as normalized analysis or validation errors.

args.title: Normalize OpenCode Events
args.content:
[Input]: Raw OpenCode global SSE events, run session context, buffered part deltas, and canonical remote-agent evidence.
[Processing]: Filter events by session, enforce primary-agent consistency, transform OpenCode status, text, reasoning, tool, question, idle, and error events into NormalizedRunEvent records, and buffer part deltas until part type is known.
[Output]: A browser-consumable stream of normalized thinking, tool_call, question, result, or error events.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Session-unrelated events are ignored; assistant text and reasoning are distinguished semantically; question and tool events are normalized for UI use; agent mismatches produce explicit runtime failure.

args.title: Handle Answers And Feedback
args.content:
[Input]: Run-specific question answers and message-feedback requests from the extension plus cached upstream question payloads and backend settings.
[Processing]: Resolve answer payloads for upstream question replies, submit answers to OpenCode question endpoints, proxy message feedback to the separate backend service, and write per-phase invocation logs.
[Output]: Accepted answer acknowledgements and accepted feedback responses aligned to the extension contract.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unknown runs return RUN_NOT_FOUND; answer submissions clear waiting question state and reach the upstream reply endpoint; feedback is proxied to the backend and invalid backend payloads are rejected.

args.content:
[Input]: Browser requests to the local test-site server and the static acceptance page assets embedded in the repository.
[Processing]: Serve the local HTML page on 127.0.0.1:4173 with deterministic DOM markers, sample username evidence, software_version, and selected_sr values used by extension capture and manual acceptance testing.

args.content: Architectural diagnosis for sidepanel message duplication: duplication persists during streaming and after completion, and survives refresh/reopen, so the defect crosses live SSE ingestion, background state synchronization, persistence, and transcript projection boundaries. Existing brownfield fit is retained in extension/src/sidepanel/**, extension/src/background/**, and extension/src/shared/**. The design decision is to harden upstream canonical event acceptance and observability rather than relying on late transcript heuristics. Stream consumption must accept only canonical ordered events, log accepted/rejected/gap/out-of-order cases, and synchronize only reconciled state. Transcript projection must consume accepted canonical events, preserve stable logical message identity and deterministic order, and emit projection anomaly diagnostics. Background synchronization must reconcile run-state snapshots using origin/frontier/version rules so stale or replayed sidepanel snapshots do not reintroduce duplicate events after refresh or reopen.

args.title: Reconcile Cross-Component Run State
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Reconcile Cross-Component Run State governs background-sidepanel snapshot reconciliation so accepted live events, persisted history, and rebroadcast state do not reintroduce duplicate, missing, or out-of-order transcript data.
[Input]: Background-stored assistant state, sidepanel synchronization payloads, active run identifiers, accepted event-frontier metadata, snapshot-origin metadata, and current browser-storage state.
[Processing]: Compare incoming run-state snapshots against the stored active-run frontier, derive a reconciliation decision per snapshot, reject stale or replayed snapshots, merge only monotonic event-frontier advances for the active run, preserve accepted events and answers already stored, and emit structured synchronization diagnostics for accepted, rejected, stale, divergent, or replayed updates.
[Output]: A reconciled canonical extension run state suitable for persistence and rebroadcast to sidepanel consumers without reintroducing duplicate, missing, or out-of-order events.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Background synchronization never overwrites a newer active-run snapshot with an older one; replayed sidepanel payloads do not duplicate accepted events after refresh or reopen; divergence between stored and incoming frontiers is logged with run id and decision outcome; rebroadcast state reflects only reconciled canonical data.

args.title: Consume Normalized Run Stream
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Consume Normalized Run Stream governs sidepanel receipt of normalized run events, canonical acceptance of those events, persistence coordination, and observability for duplicate, missing, and out-of-order stream behavior.
[Input]: Run id, adapter SSE endpoint, normalized run events, current side-panel state, prior accepted event identities, accepted ordering frontier, and current persistence/synchronization status.
[Processing]: Open the EventSource stream, validate each incoming payload, derive a canonical event key using semantic identity and stable message metadata before falling back to runId plus sequence or raw id, accept only the first event per canonical key, reject stale or replayed events that do not advance the accepted frontier, log accepted, duplicate, gap, and out-of-order decisions, persist only accepted events, update run lifecycle state from accepted evidence, and synchronize only reconciled accepted state to background.
[Output]: An updated live conversation state containing accepted ordered run events, accepted-frontier metadata, derived run status, final output snapshots, stream connection state, and structured stream diagnostics.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Semantically duplicate events are not appended twice even when raw ids differ; accepted events advance in deterministic order or are classified as out-of-order with diagnostics; missing-sequence and replay conditions are logged with run id and canonical key; terminal evidence updates run status only from accepted events; refresh or reconnect does not cause already accepted events to be re-appended.

args.title: Project Transcript Read Model
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Project Transcript Read Model governs deterministic reconstruction of sidepanel transcript messages from accepted canonical events, persisted history, and live run state without reintroducing duplicate, missing, or out-of-order assistant output.
[Input]: Historical run details, accepted live normalized events, answer records, final output snapshots, prior transcript projection state, feedback state, and accepted event-frontier metadata.
[Processing]: Build transcript messages only from accepted canonical events, preserve stable logical message identity for assistant output, order transcript parts by accepted event frontier and deterministic tie-break rules, merge historical and live segments without replaying already projected identities, restrict text-similarity coalescing to bounded presentation compaction inside the same logical message group, and emit structured diagnostics for projection anomalies such as duplicate logical groups, missing predecessor evidence, sequence regression, or message reopening after terminal state.
[Output]: A transcript projection suitable for side-panel chat rendering, summaries, and cockpit status displays, together with projection diagnostics keyed to accepted event identities.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Assistant streamed text remains distinguishable from reasoning and tool output; historical and live segments merge without duplicating accepted message groups; question and answer turns render in deterministic conversational order; projection never becomes the primary dedupe authority over raw accepted events; anomaly logs make duplicate, missing, and ordering faults traceable to their upstream source.

args.title: Persist Run History
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Persist Run History governs durable storage of canonical run artifacts so refresh, reopen, and replay use accepted event identities rather than reintroducing duplicate or unordered history.
[Input]: Run records, accepted normalized run events, answer records, session identifiers, canonical event identities, and persistence diagnostics context.
[Processing]: Persist run artifacts with stable canonical event identity metadata, upsert events idempotently for each run, preserve accepted event order and answer order, expose run detail reload APIs that return deterministic ordered artifacts, and log persistence writes, duplicate-write suppression, and ordering anomalies.
[Output]: A durable browser-side run history with per-run detail, canonical event identity metadata, answer history, and session navigation views.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Refresh or reopen replays the same accepted event set without adding duplicates; persisted events remain associated with the correct run and ordered deterministically; duplicate writes are suppressed or surfaced with diagnostics; session grouping remains stable when session ids are present.

args.title: Call Adapter API
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Call Adapter API governs extension-side HTTP and SSE integration contracts and exposes the raw transport metadata needed to diagnose duplicate, missing, or reordered run events.
[Input]: Prompt payloads, capture payloads, username context, selected agent, optional session id, feedback requests, optional API key configuration, and SSE connection context.
[Processing]: Build validated HTTP request bodies and headers for run start, answer submission, feedback submission, and SSE stream setup; parse normalized success or failure responses with schema validation; preserve raw event transport identifiers and semantic metadata provided by the adapter; surface connection-open, message, error, and reconnect transitions; and emit transport-level diagnostics needed to distinguish upstream duplication from extension-side replay.
[Output]: Typed extension-side API results, stream connections aligned to the adapter contract, and transport diagnostics for SSE lifecycle and payload identity.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Requests include authentication when configured; invalid or failed responses are normalized; stream payload parsing preserves available semantic identity fields; connection and reconnect transitions are observable in diagnostics; transport diagnostics allow the team to determine whether duplication originates before or after sidepanel acceptance.

args.title: Harden sidepanel run-stream acceptance and diagnostics in existing sidepanel modules
args.content:
Target existing modules: extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts; extension/src/sidepanel/model.ts.
Input: Active run id, parsed SSE NormalizedRunEvent payloads from createRunEventStream, current AssistantState.runEvents/currentRun/stream state, prior accepted event identities, accepted ordering frontier, and background sync state.
Processing: Introduce a canonical acceptance path before saveEvent, mergeRunEvent, and syncRunStateToBackground. Derive canonical event identity in priority order from semantic.identity, semantic.messageId+partId+channel+emissionKind, runId+sequence+type, then raw id. Reject or no-op duplicate canonical identities and stale replay that does not advance the accepted frontier. Detect gaps and out-of-order arrivals relative to the frontier. Update lifecycle state only from accepted events. Synchronize background only with accepted/reconciled state, not raw appended state.
Output: Sidepanel live state that contains only accepted canonical ordered events plus acceptance diagnostics needed for downstream persistence and background sync.
Acceptance Criteria: (1) Semantically duplicate events are not appended twice even when raw ids differ. (2) Reconnect or sidepanel reopen does not re-append already accepted events. (3) Gap and out-of-order conditions are classified without corrupting accepted event order. (4) Terminal done/error status is derived only from accepted terminal evidence. (5) Existing run-stream behavior remains functional for valid non-duplicate streams.
Observability/Logging: Emit structured logs for every received event with runId, rawEventId, canonicalEventKey, semantic.identity/messageId/partId when present, sequence, prior frontier, decision=accepted|duplicate|stale_replay|gap|out_of_order|invalid, and resulting frontier. Log every attempted background sync with accepted event count and last accepted canonical key. Log when an incoming event is ignored because it is a replay after reconnect.
Test Intent Notes: Add/extend tests for duplicate events with different raw ids but same semantic identity, stale replay after reconnect, missing-sequence/gap detection, out-of-order arrival, and proof that accepted state alone drives lifecycle transitions.

args.title: Refine transcript projection correctness and anomaly logging in reasoning timeline module
args.content:
Target existing modules: extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts.
Input: Accepted canonical live events, historical run detail events loaded from history, answer records, final output snapshots, prior TranscriptReadModel/liveProjectionState, feedback state, and accepted frontier metadata.
Processing: Rework transcript projection so it consumes accepted canonical events as source-of-truth. Preserve stable logical assistant message grouping using accepted identities. Order transcript parts deterministically by accepted frontier, then stable createdAt, then deterministic tie-break. Prevent historical/live merge from replaying already projected identities. Keep text-similarity coalescing only as bounded presentation compaction inside the same logical message group, never as primary dedupe authority. Surface projection anomalies when duplicate logical groups, sequence regression, missing predecessor evidence, or post-terminal reopening is encountered.
Output: Stable transcript read model and projection diagnostics that can be correlated back to accepted event identities and run state.
Acceptance Criteria: (1) Historical plus live projection does not duplicate assistant message groups across refresh/reopen. (2) Transcript order is deterministic for the same accepted event set. (3) Legitimate repeated assistant text in different logical groups is not incorrectly collapsed. (4) Projection anomalies are surfaced without hiding the underlying upstream problem. (5) Existing transcript UX behavior for question/answer/tool/reasoning separation remains intact.
Observability/Logging: Emit structured logs for projection start/end with runId, accepted event count, historical event count, live delta count, reusedProjectionState flag, and output message/part counts. Log anomaly records with runId, logicalMessageId/groupAnchorId, anomalyType=duplicate_group|sequence_regression|missing_predecessor|terminal_reopen|coalesced_presentation, source event ids, and chosen resolution.
Test Intent Notes: Add/extend tests covering duplicated historical/live merge, deterministic ordering under out-of-order accepted input, protection against cross-group text over-coalescing, and projection behavior after terminal state replay.

args.title: Implement background run-state reconciliation and sync observability in existing background module
args.content:
Target existing modules: extension/src/background/index.ts; supporting shared state/types used by background sync payloads.
Input: Current background-stored AssistantState, incoming SYNC_RUN_STATE payloads from sidepanel, active run id, accepted event frontier/version metadata, snapshot origin metadata, and existing stored answers/currentRun/stream status.
Processing: Replace blind overwrite behavior in syncRunState with reconciliation. Compare incoming payload against stored canonical state for the same run. Reject stale or replayed snapshots that do not advance the accepted frontier/version. Merge only monotonic advances for runEvents, answers, currentRun, stream, and status. Preserve newer stored state when incoming payload regresses. Rebroadcast STATE_UPDATED only after reconciliation and only with canonical merged state. Ensure cross-run snapshots do not clobber the active run.
Output: Canonical background state that is safe to persist and rebroadcast without reintroducing duplicate, missing, out-of-order, or stale replayed run snapshots.
Acceptance Criteria: (1) Older or replayed sidepanel payloads never overwrite newer stored active-run state. (2) Refresh/reopen of sidepanel does not cause re-sync duplication in rebroadcast state. (3) Same-run out-of-order snapshot arrival is reconciled deterministically. (4) Active run and non-active run boundaries remain intact. (5) Background rebroadcast reflects only reconciled canonical state.
Observability/Logging: Emit structured logs for each sync request with runId, origin, incoming frontier/version, stored frontier/version, decision=accepted|merged|rejected_stale|rejected_replay|ignored_cross_run, eventCount delta, answerCount delta, and rebroadcast result. Log divergence when stored and incoming payloads disagree on terminal status, pendingQuestionId, or currentRun.updatedAt.
Test Intent Notes: Add/extend tests for repeated SYNC_RUN_STATE calls, stale snapshot rejection, same-run out-of-order snapshot arrival, terminal-state regression suppression, and proof that rebroadcast after reopen does not recreate duplicates.

args.title: Add shared transport and persistence observability for stream identity and idempotent history replay
args.content:
Target existing modules: extension/src/shared/api.ts; extension/src/shared/history.ts; extension/src/shared/state.ts; extension/src/shared/types.ts; extension/src/shared/protocol.ts.
Input: EventSource lifecycle transitions, parsed SSE payloads, semantic identity fields, canonical event identity/frontier metadata, run records, event persistence requests, and history reload requests.
Processing: Extend shared transport and persistence contracts so they carry the identity metadata required by sidepanel acceptance and background reconciliation. Preserve raw transport identifiers plus semantic identity fields from parsed SSE payloads. Add replay-safe canonical event identity support to shared types. Make history persistence idempotent on canonical event identity or equivalent replay-safe key, while preserving deterministic per-run ordering on reload. Expose transport lifecycle telemetry for open, message, error, reconnect, and close transitions. Ensure reload APIs return enough metadata for transcript and reconciliation layers to correlate persisted artifacts back to accepted events.
Output: Shared contracts and persistence behavior that let implementation diagnose whether duplication, missing messages, out-of-order behavior, or stale replay originated at transport, persistence, or synchronization boundaries.
Acceptance Criteria: (1) Parsed transport data preserves available semantic identity fields needed for canonical acceptance. (2) Persisted history replay after reopen does not duplicate accepted events. (3) Persistence writes are idempotent for replay-safe event identity. (4) Reloaded run detail ordering is deterministic. (5) Shared telemetry supports transport-vs-persistence root-cause isolation.
Observability/Logging: Emit structured transport logs with runId, streamStatus transition, rawEventId, semantic.identity/messageId/partId when available, parse success/failure, reconnect count, and close reason. Emit persistence logs with runId, canonicalEventKey, writeDecision=inserted|updated|suppressed_duplicate, ordering key, and reload counts. Log parsing failures or missing identity metadata explicitly so upstream ambiguity can be diagnosed.
Test Intent Notes: Add/extend tests for EventSource lifecycle telemetry, parsing of semantic identity metadata, idempotent event persistence under replay, deterministic history reload order, and duplicate suppression when the same accepted event is written more than once.

args.title: Formal Requirement
args.content:
# Formal Requirement Draft

## Goal
Restore and formalize the Chrome extension sidepanel capability for authorized webpage capture so users can intentionally fetch webpage content by configured rules, include that captured content with their prompt/question in backend AI requests, and prevent this behavior from becoming implicit or disappearing again.

## Scope
This requirement covers:

1. **Sidepanel UI restoration**
   - Restore the sidepanel control/button that allows the user to fetch content from the active authorized webpage according to configured capture rules.
   - The control must be available in the intended sidepanel interaction path and must no longer be a hidden or accidental capability.

2. **Capture-to-run payload explicitness**
   - Make the product behavior explicit that webpage-captured data can be combined with the user’s prompt/question and then sent to the backend AI run-start flow.
   - Preserve the brownfield behavior evidenced by:
     - capture entry via `RECAPTURE` / `collectFromActiveTab`
     - orchestration path via `startRunFromActiveTab(...)`
     - backend request packaging including `prompt`, `selectedAgent`, `capture`, and `context`
     - backend adapter acceptance of `capture` and `context` in `RunStartRequest`

3. **Architecture knowledge graph explicitness**
   - Add or update architecture knowledge so this capability is documented as an intentional, traceable system behavior rather than an implicit coupling across UI, background orchestration, shared API, and backend adapter layers.

4. **Regression-proof testing**
   - Add regression tests that protect:
     - the sidepanel capture-entry UI/control presence and expected interaction
     - the flow from captured webpage data into the backend AI request payload

## Backward Compatibility & Resolved Conflicts
- **Historical context found in graph**: No relevant prior architecture requirements, constraints, or business processes were found in the knowledge graph search.
- **Conflict analysis**: No graph-documented conflict was detected.
- **Brownfield compatibility expectation**: This requirement is intended to **restore and formalize** already-evidenced brownfield behavior rather than introduce a new product concept. Existing capture-capable code paths and payload fields must remain supported and become explicitly protected by architecture documentation and tests.

## User Stories / Usage Scenarios
1. **As an extension user**, I want a visible sidepanel control to fetch content from the current authorized webpage, so I can intentionally include webpage data in my AI-assisted workflow.
2. **As an extension user**, I want captured webpage content to be sent together with my prompt/question when starting an AI run, so the backend AI can use both my instruction and the captured page evidence.
3. **As a maintainer/architect**, I want this capability explicitly represented in the architecture knowledge graph, so future changes do not accidentally remove or obscure it.
4. **As a maintainer**, I want regression tests covering both UI entry and backend payload composition, so this behavior does not silently disappear again.

## Acceptance Criteria (Mandatory)

### AC1 — Sidepanel capture control is restored
**Given** the user opens the extension sidepanel in a context where webpage capture is allowed by configured authorization/rules  
**When** the sidepanel is rendered  
**Then** a visible control for webpage capture is present in the sidepanel UI.

### AC2 — Sidepanel capture control triggers capture path
**Given** the capture control is visible in the sidepanel  
**When** the user activates the control  
**Then** the extension invokes the existing or equivalent authorized active-tab capture flow for collecting webpage data by configured rules.

### AC3 — Authorization/rule boundary is preserved
**Given** the active page is not authorized or does not satisfy configured capture rules  
**When** the user attempts to use the capture capability  
**Then** the extension must not behave as if valid page content was captured, and the UI/flow must respect the configured authorization boundary.

### AC4 — Captured webpage data is included with user prompt in run-start flow
**Given** webpage content has been captured for the active tab  
**And** the user submits a prompt/question to start an AI run  
**When** the run-start request is created  
**Then** the request payload includes the user prompt/question and the captured webpage data together in the supported run-start structure.

### AC5 — Payload structure remains explicit and traceable
**Given** a run is started from the active-tab-assisted flow  
**When** the request is sent to the backend run-start API  
**Then** traceable evidence exists in code and/or tests that the payload packaging includes the supported fields for `prompt`, `selectedAgent`, `capture`, and `context`, with `capture` representing webpage-captured data when present.

### AC6 — Backend compatibility is preserved
**Given** the backend adapter receives a run-start request containing captured webpage data  
**When** the request is validated/handled  
**Then** the request shape remains compatible with the backend model accepting `capture` and `context` in `RunStartRequest`.

### AC7 — Architecture knowledge graph is updated
**Given** this requirement is approved and implemented downstream  
**When** architecture documentation/knowledge graph updates are made  
**Then** the graph must explicitly describe:
- the sidepanel UI entry point for webpage capture
- the orchestration path that can capture page data before run start
- the request payload relationship between user prompt/question and captured webpage data
- the backend acceptance of capture-bearing run-start requests

### AC8 — Regression test for sidepanel UI entry exists
**Given** the repository test suite is executed after the change  
**When** UI regression tests run  
**Then** there is a test that fails if the sidepanel capture control/entry capability disappears again.

### AC9 — Regression test for capture-to-backend payload exists
**Given** the repository test suite is executed after the change  
**When** flow/integration/unit tests covering run-start payload creation execute  
**Then** there is a test that fails if captured webpage data is no longer packaged together with the user prompt/question for the backend request.

### AC10 — Evidence remains inspectable for future maintainers
**Given** a future maintainer inspects the repository and architecture artifacts  
**When** they trace this capability  
**Then** they can identify the UI entry, orchestration path, payload packaging, backend contract, and regression tests without relying on tribal knowledge.

## Constraints & Non-functional Requirements
- Do not define this as an implicit behavior only discoverable from code; it must be explicit in architecture knowledge.
- The requirement is for a **brownfield restoration/formalization**, so downstream design should preserve verified existing payload semantics unless an explicit architectural change is approved later.
- Regression coverage must be durable enough to detect both:
  - UI removal/regression
  - payload composition regression
- The capability must remain bounded by configured authorization/capture rules for webpage access.
- The requirement must support end-to-end traceability across UI, orchestration, API packaging, backend contract, and tests.

## Open Questions / Assumptions
- Assumption: “authorized webpage by configured rules” refers to the repository’s existing authorization/capture-rule mechanism and does not require new policy design in this requirement.
- Assumption: The exact button label may vary by product language/UI copy, but the user-visible capture entry itself is mandatory.
- Assumption: The architecture graph update may be implemented through one or more linked architecture elements, as long as the capability becomes explicit and traceable.

  - UI removal/regression
  - payload composition regression
- The capability must remain bounded by configured authorization/capture rules for webpage access.
- The requirement must support end-to-end traceability across UI, orchestration, API packaging, backend contract, and tests.

## Open Questions / Assumptions
- Assumption: “authorized webpage by configured rules” refers to the repository’s existing authorization/capture-rule mechanism and does not require new policy design in this requirement.
- Assumption: The exact button label may vary by product language/UI copy, but the user-visible capture entry itself is mandatory.
- Assumption: The architecture graph update may be implemented through one or more linked architecture elements, as long as the capability becomes explicit and traceable.

Implementation handoff is decomposed into function-level tasks with regression expectations for sidepanel capture entry visibility, recapture dispatch, capture-plus-prompt payload packaging, and backend compatibility.

args.title: Authorized Webpage Capture Run-Start Service
args.content: An ApplicationService represents externally visible automated behavior offered by application components to support business behavior. In this architecture, Authorized Webpage Capture Run-Start Service makes explicit the end-to-end capability in which the sidepanel exposes a visible capture entry, the extension captures authorized active-page data according to configured rules, and the run-start flow preserves that captured data together with the user prompt/question in the backend request contract.

args.title: Expose Sidepanel Capture Entry
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Expose Sidepanel Capture Entry governs the visible operator-facing sidepanel control that preserves webpage capture as an intentional UI action and dispatches recapture into the extension flow.
[Input]: User interaction with the sidepanel composer area, current assistant busy state, and the visible capture-control rendering state.
[Processing]: Render and preserve an explicit sidepanel capture control, prevent invocation while the assistant is busy, and dispatch the recapture command through the controller so capture remains an intentional operator action rather than an implicit side effect.
[Output]: A visible capture entry in the sidepanel UI and a dispatched recapture request when the user activates it.
[Acceptance Criteria]: The rendered sidepanel includes a visible capture control in the intended interaction path; activating the control dispatches the recapture flow; regression tests fail if the control disappears or no longer dispatches recapture; the UI does not bypass existing authorization boundaries.

args.title: Orchestrate Authorized Capture And Run Start
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Orchestrate Authorized Capture And Run Start governs the background-side orchestration that enforces authorization and rules, collects webpage data when requested, and preserves capture-aware run-start handoff semantics.
[Input]: User prompt/question, selected main agent, optional session id, capture-page intent, active-tab metadata, stored rules, and runtime authorization state.
[Processing]: Enforce active-tab authorization and configured rule boundaries, optionally collect page fields from the authorized active tab, derive username context, preserve matched-rule state, and pass the resulting prompt plus captured webpage data into the shared run-start packaging flow without changing the supported payload semantics.
[Output]: An authorized capture result and a run-start handoff containing prompt, selected agent, optional captured webpage data, and context inputs for request packaging.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unauthorized or unmatched pages do not produce a false successful capture; authorized pages can still capture and start a run; regression tests fail if captured webpage data stops flowing from background orchestration into run-start handoff; existing payload semantics remain compatible with prompt, selectedAgent, capture, and context.

args.title: Package Capture-Enriched Run-Start Request
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Package Capture-Enriched Run-Start Request governs the shared API behavior that builds the backend /api/runs request so captured webpage data remains explicitly packaged together with the user prompt/question when present.
[Input]: User prompt/question, selected main agent, optional captured webpage data, username context, and optional session id.
[Processing]: Normalize optional capture content, build the run-start HTTP request body, preserve the supported fields prompt and selectedAgent, include capture when present, and always include context metadata compatible with the backend contract.
[Output]: A POST /api/runs request payload containing prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture, optional sessionId, and context.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Regression tests fail if captured webpage data is no longer packaged together with the user prompt/question when present; send-only flows still omit capture correctly; payload structure remains inspectable and compatible with the existing run-start contract; tests verify prompt, selectedAgent, capture, and context semantics.

args.title: Accept Capture-Bearing RunStartRequest
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Accept Capture-Bearing RunStartRequest governs backend request-model acceptance so capture-bearing run-start payloads remain compatible with the adapter's existing request validation and startup flow.
[Input]: Backend POST /api/runs request containing prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture payload, optional sessionId, and context metadata.
[Processing]: Validate the incoming RunStartRequest model, preserve backend compatibility for capture and context fields, and hand the accepted request to adapter run-start processing without dropping capture-bearing payload support.
[Output]: A validated backend run-start request accepted for adapter processing or a normalized validation failure.
[Acceptance Criteria]: RunStartRequest continues to accept capture and context; regression tests fail if backend acceptance removes either field or breaks capture-bearing /api/runs requests; accepted requests remain compatible with the existing adapter start-run flow.

args.title: Restore visible sidepanel webpage-capture entry in the Extension Side Panel Experience
args.content:
Target existing module(s): extension/src/sidepanel/App.tsx; extension/src/sidepanel/components/composer/Composer.tsx; extension/src/sidepanel/useSidepanelController.ts; extension/src/sidepanel/App.test.tsx.

Test intent notes: Use sidepanel/App-level UI regression coverage to assert visible presence of the capture entry and runtimeSendMessage({ type: 'RECAPTURE' }) dispatch on click; keep architecture/requirement trace tags in the test file so future audits can trace this requirement.

args.title: Preserve authorized capture-to-run orchestration in the Extension Background Orchestrator
args.content:
Target existing module(s): extension/src/background/index.ts; extension/src/background/index.test.ts.
[Input]: Existing collectFromActiveTab(), runCaptureOnly(), startRunFromActiveTab(... capturePageData?: boolean), getRules(), authorization/permission checks, matched-rule state handling, and current background tests for capture and send-only flows.
[Processing]: Preserve background ownership of authorization and rule enforcement, ensure capturePageData-controlled orchestration still collects authorized page data and username context when requested, keep prompt/capture handoff flowing into startRun(...), and refine/add regression tests that fail if captured webpage data stops being forwarded into the downstream run-start request path or if unauthorized/unmatched pages are treated as valid captures.
[Output]: A background orchestration flow that preserves rule/permission boundaries and continues handing prompt plus optional captured webpage data into backend run-start packaging.
[Acceptance Criteria]: Unauthorized or rule-unmatched pages do not produce a successful capture-bearing run start; authorized pages with capturePageData=true still collect fields and hand captured data into startRun packaging; existing send-only flows with capturePageData=false remain valid and do not perform unwanted capture; regression tests fail if the capture-aware handoff disappears or if authorization boundaries are bypassed.
Test intent notes: Use background/index.test.ts coverage to assert both negative and positive paths: no-rule/no-auth failures, authorized capture collection, and serialized /api/runs body evidence that captured page data remains coupled to run startup when capturePageData=true.

args.title: Preserve capture-enriched /api/runs payload packaging in the Extension Shared Contract Layer
args.content:
Target existing module(s): extension/src/shared/api.ts; extension/src/shared/api.test.ts.
[Input]: Existing startRun(prompt, capture, usernameContext, selectedAgent, sessionId?) implementation, fetch serialization logic, RunStartRequest-compatible payload shape, and current shared API tests for capture-bearing and capture-free requests.
[Processing]: Preserve the shared API contract that builds POST /api/runs payloads, keep prompt and selectedAgent mandatory, include capture when normalizedCapture is present, always include context metadata, and refine/add regression tests that inspect the actual serialized request body so future changes cannot silently drop capture or decouple it from the user prompt/question.
[Output]: A concrete, inspectable backend request payload contract containing prompt, selectedAgent, optional capture, optional sessionId, and context, plus regression tests that verify both capture-bearing and prompt-only paths.
[Acceptance Criteria]: A capture-bearing shared API test fails if the serialized request body no longer includes prompt, selectedAgent, capture, and context together; prompt-only requests still omit capture correctly while preserving context; pageTitle/pageUrl context propagation remains compatible with the existing contract; maintainers can inspect tests to trace exactly where capture+prompt packaging is enforced.
Test intent notes: Use shared/api.test.ts to assert JSON.parse(fetchMock.mock.calls[0][1].body) contains the expected capture-bearing fields and that send-only flows omit capture without altering prompt/context semantics; keep architecture/requirement tags for audit traceability.

args.content: Full-model refactor approved for Sidepanel transcript projection and renderer. Within ELM-COMP-EXT-SIDEPANEL, redesign the conversation projection as a state-machine-driven Assistant Message Node model that decouples accepted SSE events from UI transcript grouping, keeps reasoning/tool/text inside one stable assistant node until terminal state, prevents synthetic User node creation from stream-status changes or empty payloads, and introduces an incremental renderer contract that updates only the active assistant tail while preserving historical node identity and avoiding whole-list rerender or full markdown reparse on each token.

args.title: Render Incremental Transcript Tail
args.content:
[Input]: Stable transcript read model, active assistant node identity, structural patch notifications, tail delta payloads, feedback UI state, auto-follow state, and transcript action metadata.
[Processing]: Render transcript messages from the read-model contract instead of raw SSE events; keep sealed messages referentially stable; accumulate high-frequency assistant text deltas in refs or equivalent buffers; flush tail updates through debounce, requestAnimationFrame, or memoized local patch application; update only the active assistant message tail and defer markdown reparsing to the affected active block; preserve precise DOM bubble counts and action affordances when assistant phases alternate between reasoning, tool, and text.
[Output]: Incrementally updated transcript DOM with stable historical message subtrees, locally updated active assistant tail, accurate final markdown, and scroll behavior that follows live updates without whole-list rerender churn.
[Acceptance Criteria]: GIVEN SSE emits single-character tokens at very high frequency such as every 10ms, WHEN React renders the stream, THEN ref buffering plus debounce or memoization keeps the UI smooth and preserves exact markdown output without omissions; each token does not trigger whole transcript rerender or full markdown reparse; DOM never shows more than the intended user and assistant bubbles for a single turn under tool/text alternation.

args.title: Project Transcript Read Model
args.content:
An ApplicationFunction represents automated application behavior grouped by required application resources or competencies. In this architecture, Project Transcript Read Model owns the deterministic projection of accepted live and historical run events into stable conversation message nodes and renderer-ready transcript state for the Sidepanel.
[Input]: Accepted canonical normalized SSE run events, historical run segments, live run segment, prompt and answer records, final output snapshots, terminal status fields, feedback state, prior live projection state, and prior stable transcript identities.
[Processing]: Maintain an explicit conversation projection state machine that separates raw event flow from UI transcript grouping; create User Message Nodes only from verified user prompt or answer sources; bind assistant reasoning, tool_call, and text emissions to one stable Assistant Message Node using semantic.messageId or data.message_id with provisional run-scoped fallback; prevent assistant tool/text status transitions, empty payloads, or out-of-order SSE injections from opening synthetic User nodes; finalize assistant nodes only on terminal state or verified next-user-turn boundaries; emit structural and tail-oriented projection metadata while preserving historical node identity and anomaly diagnostics.
[Output]: Stable transcript read model containing deterministic message nodes, active assistant node identity, sealed historical segments, tail-update metadata, summary state, and anomaly diagnostics suitable for renderer consumption.
[Acceptance Criteria]: GIVEN a long streaming assistant answer with reasoning, tool_call, and final text phases, WHEN SSE status switches between tool and text, THEN the projection preserves one Assistant Message ID and renders no empty User bubble; out-of-order or empty SSE payloads do not create new User nodes; assistant nodes remain open until terminal state; historical transcript identity remains stable while live tail updates incrementally.

args.title: Sidepanel 会话体验重构：达到 OpenCode Web 级别的丝滑流式交互体验
args.content:
# Requirement: ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX
## 名称
Sidepanel 会话体验重构：达到 OpenCode Web 级别的丝滑流式交互体验

## Goal
对当前浏览器插件 Sidepanel 的会话界面进行一次深度 UI/UX 架构重构，目标不是局部视觉修补，而是建立可持续演进的高性能会话渲染架构，使其在主观体验、交互流畅度、流式输出节奏、滚动行为与信息分层上对标 OpenCode Web 的顶级会话体验。

## 核心架构性要求
### R1. 组件级读写隔离
- 历史消息必须封装为独立纯渲染组件。
- 已完成消息必须通过稳定 props 与 React.memo 或等价冻结策略避免重复渲染。
- 当前仍在流式输出的消息必须作为独立的 Active Tail 渲染单元存在。
- Active Tail 的更新不得触发历史消息列表父级的整体重新渲染。
- 会话状态管理必须体现“稳定历史区”与“动态流式区”的读写隔离。

### R2. Markdown 增量与异步渲染优化
- 禁止在每次 SSE delta 到达时对整段 Markdown 执行全量同步解析。
- 流式阶段必须采用 requestAnimationFrame、节流器或等价机制控制渲染频率，上限不得高于 30fps。
- 在 terminal state 到达时，必须执行最终强制 flush，确保最终展示内容完整一致。

### R3. 智能滚动控制
- 必须实现 pin-to-bottom 行为。
- 用户手动向上滚动脱离底部后，系统必须暂停自动滚动，并显示“⬇ 最新消息”悬浮提示。
- 用户点击该提示后，视图应恢复到底部并重新进入自动跟随态。

### R4. 思考过程与结果分离渲染
- Reasoning、Tool Call、Final Answer 必须在视觉上明确区分。
- Reasoning / Tool Call 必须支持折叠/展开。
- Final Answer 必须默认处于最易阅读的主展示层。

## Acceptance Criteria
- 历史消息在流式期间不重渲染，仅 Active Tail 更新。
- 流式渲染必须经由 rAF/节流调度，实际 UI 提交频率不得高于 30fps。
- terminal state 到达时必须立即最终 flush，最终展示内容完整一致。
- 用户上滚时不得被强制拉回，且必须显示“⬇ 最新消息”提示。
- Reasoning / Tool Call / Final Answer 必须清晰分层，且思考过程支持折叠/展开。

args.content: Full-model design prepared for Requirement ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX. Within existing extension/src/sidepanel boundary, refactor Sidepanel conversation UX into stable historical transcript zone, isolated Active Tail renderer, independent scroll-follow controller, and reasoning/result separated presentation. Extend reasoningTimeline.ts as strict transcript projection seam, preserve useSidepanelController as orchestration-only layer, and split reasoningTimelineView.tsx into ConversationViewport, HistoricalTranscriptList, ActiveTailRenderer, ScrollFollowController, and ProcessDisclosure/FinalAnswerPanel. Core constraints: historical messages frozen via stable props and React.memo, only Active Tail may update during streaming, markdown rendering must be rAF/throttle controlled with max 30fps and terminal force flush, and detached scrolling must suspend auto-follow while exposing a latest-message affordance.

args.content: Issue-driven architecture refinement for ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX sidepanel transcript flow after human-authoritative DOM defect escalation. Strict contract: for one runId, all assistant SSE phases (reasoning, tool_call, tool_result/result, text deltas) must aggregate into exactly one logical Assistant Message Node and one physical <article class="transcript-message-assistant">. Empty user nodes are forbidden. Existing software-unit boundaries are retained: SU-SP-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION owns run-scoped assistant aggregation in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts; SU-SP-CONVERSATION-VIEWPORT owns the single assistant DOM boundary and internal layering in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx. ProcessDisclosure must render reasoning/tools via collapsed-by-default <details>; FinalAnswerPanel must own incremental final-text rendering without assistant-article remount.

args.content: ModelUpdated: Human-approved architecture rework for sidepanel transcript rendering defect. No new software units introduced. Reuse TASK-018 as retry target for SU-SP-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION and TASK-022 as retry target for SU-SP-CONVERSATION-VIEWPORT because the issue is a contract refinement within existing ownership boundaries, not a decomposition change. args.content: Full-model architecture compliance audit passed for sidepanel transcript rework at commit c3c6259351e895bf96a4071bd247c75b2d18d153. Intent baseline ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX remains aligned with code reality for TASK-018 and TASK-022. TASK-018 acceptable no-change verification confirmed: extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts was unchanged in the audited commit and still retains SU-SP-TRANSCRIPT-PROJECTION ownership plus single-run assistant aggregation via buildStableTranscriptProjection. TASK-022 implementation in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx preserves a single assistant article boundary, moves ProcessDisclosure to native collapsed-by-default <details>, keeps FinalAnswerPanel as owner of incremental final text rendering, and avoids assistant article remount churn across streaming updates. Tagged automated verification for ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX is present and scanner-verified in reasoningTimeline.test.ts, reasoningTimelineView.test.tsx, and reasoningTimeline.chromeSandbox.test.tsx. Targeted extension tests passed (54 tests) and extension typecheck passed.

args.content: QA passed for ELM-REQ-OPENCODE-UX at commit c3c6259351e895bf96a4071bd247c75b2d18d153. Validated TASK-018 and TASK-022 with targeted executed tests in extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.test.ts, extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.test.tsx, and extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.chromeSandbox.test.tsx (54 tests passed) plus extension workspace typecheck passed. Executable evidence covers one-run one-user/one-assistant article boundary, empty user answer suppression, collapsed-by-default native details disclosure, FinalAnswerPanel incremental ownership, anti-fragmentation DOM count, and assistant article stability across tail updates/finalization. run_chrome_sandbox was attempted but blocked by ERR_UNKNOWN_FILE_EXTENSION on the .tsx entry script, so sandbox-tool execution was not available; sandbox-capable coverage still executed locally via the chrome sandbox test file under Vitest/jsdom. 跳过了真实环境验证 because live_environment_ready=false.

args.title: Transcript Tail Patch
args.content:
当前活动 assistant 消息的局部增量渲染补丁。证据：extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts:2681-2694；extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx:403-506。
核心字段：activeMessageId, anchorId, fullText, deltaText, updatedAt, revision, terminal。用于仅刷新 active tail，而不重渲染历史消息列表。

args.title: Conversation Transcript Viewport Interface
args.content:
Sidepanel 会话主舞台渲染接口。证据：extension/src/sidepanel/components/stage/MainStage.tsx:116-139；extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimelineView.tsx:577-667, 698-938。
[Input]: transcriptReadModel 派生出的 sealedMessages、processParts、finalAnswerPart、questionPart、errorPart、summaryPart、tailPatch、terminalState，以及 disclosure/scroll/feedback/retry/question-submit 回调。
[Processing]: 先渲染历史消息列表，再维持单个活动 assistant article；通过 ProcessDisclosure 展示 reasoning/tool，FinalAnswerPanel 展示最终回答，LatestMessageButton 控制脱底后的回到底部。
[Output]: 稳定的 transcript DOM，关键边界为单个 <article class="transcript-message-assistant"> 活动 assistant 气泡。
